channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Stronger application-layer types remove impossible states
item.description: Today I completed a small Company write vertical slice in TypeScript, from business validation to PostgreSQL persistence. One detail clarified why application services are more than repository...

item.title: LLD Domain Modeling: The Complete Mental Framework (How to Think in Any System Design Problem)
item.description: This is the final consolidation of everything in Domain Modeling. If you understand this structure deeply, you don’t need to memorize LLD patterns anymore. You can derive them. The Core Idea of...

item.title: The Bug Was in the Grader
item.description: The most expensive bug I shipped last month was not in a model, and it was not in a prompt. It was in the thing doing the grading. I found it three times, in three different businesses, in the...

item.title: OpenEvidence is bribing spouses of CMI and Genentech leadership
item.description: OpenEvidence is bribing spouses of CMI and Genentech leadership to block competitors Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48959765 Points: 3 # Comments: 0

item.title: I Built a Password Strength Checker That Actually Works (Here's Why Most Fail)
item.description: Here's the dirty secret of password security: those little bars that turn green when you add an exclamation mark or a number are basically fancy lies. You know the drill"Password must be 8+...

item.title: Apache Data Lakehouse Weekly: July 11 to July 18, 2026
item.description: The open lakehouse stack spent this week arguing about what belongs in a spec and what belongs in a release. Iceberg contributors opened a formal push to remove equality deletes from the V4 spec,...

item.title: Google is open-sourcing its 3D emoji
item.description: Now, if you want to, you can use Google's 3D emoji in your own creations. The company shared some details about how it went about designing the little pictograms and why, as part of World Emoji...

item.title: Instrumenting a Go API with Prometheus
item.description: Exposing a /metrics endpoint takes five minutes. Getting the instrumentation itself right — metrics that stay cheap at scale, catch real problems, and don't lie under load. Here's a breakdown of...

item.title: Introducing Light — A New Programming Language
item.description: Over the past 9 months, I've been designing and developing Light, a programming language with the long-term vision of making script automation more structured, consistent, and easier to build and...

item.title: geomap-engine: fusing AR pose + object detections into a 2D map, in Rust
item.description: Every AR toolkit (ARKit, ARCore, RoomPlan, Polycam) bundles SLAM, object detection, and a UI into one closed, heavyweight package. If you just want the middle piece — turn a stream of {camera...

item.title: HI !! Let's start OOP simply.
item.description: OOPاول ما بنبدأ نتعلم ::بنقابل كلمتين مشهورين جدا وهما CLASSES OBJECTS ؟؟؟👀👀OOPطب ما تيجوا فى البداية نعرف يعنى ايه ال :::يسيدى يعنى OOP ”programming paradigm based on concept of “OBJECTS” “ . OR...

item.title: GoPro’s discounted Max 2 bundle includes $100 worth of accessories
item.description: A 360-degree camera is a great way to ensure you capture every bit of the action, but prices tend to be on the high end for models worth your attention. That’s why it’s notable that the GoPro Max...

item.title: The Guardian’s Carter Sherman fondly remembers being terrified by Ocarina of Time
item.description: Carter Sherman has been covering sex, gender, and the complex personal and national politics that accompany them for years. She was a senior reporter for Vice and has written for Elle, Ms....

item.title: Surprise! Facial recognition smart locks are actually good
item.description: Hands-free unlocking is the future of smart locks. The best smart home tech removes friction, and having your door unlock for you as you walk up is as frictionless as it gets - no passcodes to...

item.title: Sony’s flagship RGB LED TV is incredible
item.description: The Sony Bravia 9 II is the most anticipated new TV in years. It's an amazing RGB LED TV. I watched Dungeons & Dragons: Honor Among Thieves on the new Bravia with my son, who has been getting into...

item.title: More games should be on rails (literally)
item.description: It's been a good few weeks for games on rails. Nintendo's Star Fox remake wisely kept the tightly scripted, action-packed levels from Star Fox 64 largely the same, and they're still fun to fly...

item.title: The apps, gadgets, and tools every reader needs
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 136, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, hope your neighborhood isn't as smoky as mine, and also you can...

item.title: Your Period Tracker Is (Probably) Spying on You
item.description: Plus: Russian cyberspies turn to infrastructure hacking, DHS repeatedly fails to realize it’d been hacked, a breach exposes an AI music generator’s scraping ways, and more.

item.title: How Google’s New Gemini Rates Work and How to Track Your Usage
item.description: Now that Google has changed how its usage quotas are tallied, you might not get as many AI responses as you did before.

item.title: Prompt Injection Attacks Are Thwarting AI Hacking Agents
item.description: “Context bombing” tricks malicious AI agents into shutting down before they can do harm.

item.title: Fine, electric mountain bikes don’t suck
item.description: Cheater, I'd grumble between huffs as yet another e-bike rider casually skittered past me on a steep ascent. It's this purist attitude that, for years, has left me blind to one simple fact:...

item.title: Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out
item.description: Neil Rimer, the venture capitalist who co-founded Index Ventures, predicts the historic wealth AI is generating in Silicon Valley will have to be redistributed, voluntarily or involuntarily.

item.title: Vertu wants executives to pay $6,880 for an AI agent — here’s how it actually performs
item.description: From AI workflows to battery life and security, here's what it's really like to live with Vertu's luxury foldable every day.

item.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
item.description: Databricks has remade its image into an AI company and has published research on the cost savings of open weight AI models for coding.

item.title: Taylor Farms pulls iceberg lettuce from the US market after cyclosporiasis outbreak
item.description: Food producer Taylor Farms released a statement on the Cyclospora outbreak Friday, confirming that it's "voluntarily removing all iceberg lettuce sourced from central Mexico from the US market."...

item.title: The Zoom hack that says, ‘Don’t record me’
item.description: If every meeting, watercooler conversation, and date gets transcribed and summarized, who's actually reading any of it?

item.title: New wp2shell WordPress Core Flaw Lets Unauthenticated Attackers Run Code
item.description: Updated July 18, 2026: the two flaws now carry CVE IDs, the full mechanism has been published, a persistent-object-cache condition has surfaced, and a working proof-of-concept is public. The story...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Squid Washing Up on Cape Cod Beach
item.description: Lots of articles about this. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered. Blog moderation policy.

item.title: OpenSSL HollowByte Flaw Could Freeze Server Memory with 11-Byte TLS Requests
item.description: Eleven bytes will make an unpatched OpenSSL server set aside up to 131 KB of memory for a message that never arrives. On the glibc systems Okta tested, that memory is gone until the process...

item.title: Agility Robotics plants its flag in Tesla’s backyard
item.description: Agility is opening a new training center for its Digit robots in Fremont, California.
